question.text: Under PMLA, a "reporting entity" includes all of the following EXCEPT:

answer.text: Intermediaries registered under Section 12 of SEBI Act
Individual retail investors in the stock market

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Section 2(wa) of PMLA defines "reporting entity" as a banking company, financial institution, intermediary, or a person carrying on a designated business or profession. Individual retail investors are NOT reporting entities — they are clients of reporting entities.
**The Trap:** All three legitimate options (banking companies, financial institutions, intermediaries) are explicitly included in the definition. The exam tests whether you can distinguish between reporting entities (who report) and their clients (about whom reports are made).
**Cross-Exam Context:** In NISM III-A, intermediaries are tested on their obligations AS reporting entities. In NISM XXIV, the broader definition covering banking companies and designated businesses is also relevant.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Persons carrying on "designated business or profession" include those involved in casinos, real estate agents, dealers in precious metals/stones, and persons engaged in safekeeping of cash/securities — these are also reporting entities under PMLA.
